name: Russia–Ukraine War
summary: Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 followed years of escalating tensions rooted in Ukraine's post-Soviet identity struggle and Russia's opposition to NATO expansion.

history.event: Ukraine declares independence after Soviet collapse; Russia reluctantly accepts
Budapest Memorandum: Ukraine gives up nuclear weapons in exchange for security guarantees from Russia, US, UK
Orange Revolution: mass protests overturn rigged election favouring pro-Russia candidate Yanukovych
Yanukovych wins presidency; tilts Ukraine toward Russia, away from EU
Euromaidan protests erupt when Yanukovych rejects EU deal; he flees to Russia
Russia annexes Crimea after disputed referendum; West imposes initial sanctions
Pro-Russian separatists seize Donetsk and Luhansk; war in Donbas begins
Minsk I ceasefire agreement signed; repeatedly violated
Minsk II agreement signed; heavy weaponry withdrawal never fully implemented
Zelensky elected president on peace platform; negotiations stall
Russia masses 100,000+ troops near Ukraine border; issues ultimatum to NATO
Russia launches full-scale invasion; attacks Kyiv, Kharkiv, Mariupol simultaneously
Russia fails to take Kyiv; withdraws, shifts focus to eastern and southern Ukraine
Bucha massacre revealed after Russian withdrawal; war crimes investigations begin
Ukraine launches Kharkiv counteroffensive; recaptures 6,000 sq km in weeks
Ukraine recaptures Kherson city; Russia retreats east of Dnipro River
Ukraine's summer counteroffensive makes limited gains; war becomes stalemated trench warfare
US Congress stalls on new Ukraine aid package; ammunition shortages worsen
Russia captures Avdiivka after months of siege; Ukraine retreats
US Congress finally passes $61bn Ukraine aid package after 6-month delay
Continued drone warfare, deep strikes inside Russia; frontlines shift slowly; no ceasefire in sight

name: Israel–Hamas Conflict
summary: The October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Israeli military operation in Gaza are the latest chapter in a conflict rooted in the 1948 Arab-Israeli war and decades of failed peace processes.

history.event: Israel declares independence; Arab-Israeli war displaces 700,000 Palestinians (the Nakba)
Six-Day War: Israel occupies West Bank, Gaza, Sinai, and Golan Heights
Yom Kippur War; Egypt and Syria attack Israel; ceasefire brokered by US and USSR
First Intifada: Palestinian uprising in occupied territories; Hamas founded
Oslo Accords: PLO recognises Israel; Israel recognises PLO; two-state framework agreed
Camp David summit collapses; Second Intifada begins; suicide bombings escalate
Israel unilaterally withdraws from Gaza; settlements removed
Hamas wins Palestinian elections; West refuses to recognise Hamas government
Hamas seizes control of Gaza from Fatah; Israel imposes blockade
Operation Cast Lead: Israel launches major Gaza offensive after rocket fire
Operation Pillar of Defence: 8-day conflict; ceasefire brokered by Egypt
Operation Protective Edge: 50-day war; over 2,000 Gazans killed; tunnels discovered
11-day war after Jerusalem tensions; ceasefire brokered by Egypt and Qatar
Hamas attacks southern Israel; 1,200 killed, 250 taken hostage — deadliest day for Jews since Holocaust
Israel declares war; launches air campaign and ground invasion of Gaza
Temporary ceasefire; 105 hostages released in exchange for Palestinian prisoners
Fighting resumes; significant civilian casualties; humanitarian crisis deepens
ICJ orders Israel to prevent genocidal acts; does not order ceasefire
Rafah offensive threatened; US warns against major ground operation
Negotiations for permanent ceasefire and hostage deal continue; humanitarian catastrophe ongoing
Post-war governance of Gaza unresolved; reconstruction and Palestinian statehood questions unanswered

name: US–China Tech & Trade War
summary: A systemic rivalry between the world's two largest economies over trade, technology supremacy, and geopolitical influence, intensifying since 2018 and increasingly focused on semiconductors and Taiwan.

history.event: China joins WTO; massive export growth begins; US manufacturing declines
China surpasses US as world's largest manufacturer; trade deficit with US widens
China launches 'Made in China 2025' plan to dominate strategic tech sectors
Trump administration identifies China as 'strategic competitor' in National Security Strategy
US imposes first round of tariffs ($50bn) on Chinese goods; China retaliates
Escalating tariff war; over $360bn of Chinese goods hit with US tariffs
Huawei added to US Entity List; banned from US technology; allies pressured to exclude
Phase One trade deal signed; China pledges to buy $200bn more US goods — never fulfilled
COVID-19 deepens rivalry; supply chain vulnerabilities exposed; decoupling accelerates
Biden maintains Trump tariffs; frames rivalry as 'democracy vs autocracy'
CHIPS and Science Act signed; $52bn for US semiconductor manufacturing
US imposes sweeping export controls on advanced chips and chipmaking equipment to China
Pelosi visits Taiwan; China conducts large military exercises around island
Netherlands and Japan join US chip export restrictions; ASML halts sales to China
US restricts outbound investment in Chinese AI, quantum, semiconductors
Biden imposes 100% tariff on Chinese EVs, 50% on solar panels, 25% on steel
Trump elected; signals even more aggressive tariff regime
Further tariff escalation; both sides deepen parallel tech ecosystems; decoupling accelerates

name: India–Pakistan Tensions
summary: A nuclear-armed rivalry since partition in 1947, centred on Kashmir. Three full wars, multiple crises, and ongoing cross-border tensions define one of the world's most dangerous bilateral relationships.

history.event: British India partitioned; millions displaced; Kashmir's accession to India contested
First Kashmir War; UN resolution calls for plebiscite — never held
Second Indo-Pakistani War over Kashmir; ceasefire after 17 days
Third war: India backs Bangladesh independence; Pakistan loses East Pakistan
India occupies Siachen Glacier; world's highest battlefield
Both nations test nuclear weapons; become declared nuclear powers
Kargil War: Pakistan-backed forces infiltrate Indian territory; India repels after 60 days
Parliament attack in New Delhi; India blames Pakistan; nuclear standoff
Mumbai attacks: 166 killed; perpetrators linked to Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba; talks collapse
Uri attack; India conducts 'surgical strikes' across Line of Control
Pulwama attack kills 40 Indian soldiers; India launches airstrikes in Balakot; aerial dogfight
India revokes Article 370; Jammu & Kashmir's special status removed; Pakistan protests
Diplomatic ties remain frozen; trade suspended; no ambassadors exchanged
Cross-border firing incidents along LoC increase; intelligence agencies accuse each other
Exchange of fire; diplomatic envoys summoned; both sides place forces on alert
Tensions remain elevated; no normalisation in sight; proxy accusations continue

name: Sudan Civil War
summary: A devastating civil war between Sudan's army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces has created one of the world's worst humanitarian crises, with origins in decades of military rule and the Darfur conflict.

history.event: Omar al-Bashir seizes power in military coup; Islamist government installed
Darfur conflict begins; Arab militias (Janjaweed) attack non-Arab villages; 300,000+ killed
ICC indicts Bashir for war crimes and genocide — first sitting head of state indicted
South Sudan secedes after referendum; Sudan loses 75% of oil revenue
Bashir overthrown after mass protests; military and civilians share power in transitional council
RSF commander Hemedti becomes deputy head of transitional council alongside SAF chief Al-Burhan
Military coup; Al-Burhan and Hemedti dissolve civilian government
International pressure for return to civilian rule; Framework Agreement signed but implementation fails
Fighting erupts between SAF and RSF in Khartoum; hundreds killed in first week
US and Saudi Arabia broker short ceasefires; all collapse within days
RSF commits mass atrocities in El Geneina, Darfur; hundreds executed
Over 5 million displaced; famine conditions in parts of Darfur
RSF advances; captures most of Khartoum; SAF retreats to Omdurman
8+ million displaced; UN calls it world's largest displacement crisis; no ceasefire

name: Iran Nuclear & Regional Tensions
summary: Iran's nuclear programme and regional proxy strategy have kept the Middle East on edge for decades. The collapse of the JCPOA and the Gaza war have brought tensions to a new high.

history.event: Islamic Revolution; Khomeini establishes theocratic republic; US hostage crisis
Iran-Iraq War; Saddam Hussein backed by US; over 500,000 killed
Iran's secret nuclear programme revealed; IAEA inspections begin
Iran enriches uranium for first time; UN Security Council imposes sanctions
Stuxnet cyberattack (US/Israel) destroys Iranian centrifuges
Crippling oil sanctions; Iran's currency collapses; nuclear talks resume
JCPOA signed: Iran limits enrichment in exchange for sanction relief
Trump withdraws from JCPOA; reimpose 'maximum pressure' sanctions
Iran attacks tankers in Gulf; shoots down US drone; tensions near war
US kills IRGC General Soleimani in Baghdad drone strike; Iran retaliates with missiles
Iran accelerates enrichment; JCPOA talks stall under Biden
Iran supplies Russia with Shahed drones for use in Ukraine
IAEA reports Iran has enriched uranium to 83.7% — near weapons-grade
Post-Gaza war: Iran-backed Hezbollah, Houthis, and Iraqi militias launch attacks on Israel and US forces
Iran launches first-ever direct attack on Israel: 300+ drones and missiles (mostly intercepted)
Nuclear talks stalled; Israel and US consider military options; Houthi attacks on shipping continue

name: US Political Landscape
summary: American democracy has entered a period of intense polarisation, institutional stress, and contested norms, accelerated by the Trump presidency, COVID, and the 2020 election dispute.

history.event: Obama elected first Black president; Democrats win both houses
Tea Party wave; Republicans retake House; gridlock begins
Trump defeats Clinton; shocks political establishment; populist wave emerges
Mueller investigation into Russian election interference; multiple convictions
Trump impeached by House for Ukraine pressure; acquitted by Senate
COVID-19 pandemic mismanaged; 1 million Americans die over following years
Biden defeats Trump; Trump refuses to concede; pushes baseless fraud claims
Trump supporters storm US Capitol; 5 die; world shocked; 1,000+ charged
Trump impeached again (incitement); acquitted by Senate
Biden passes infrastructure and climate bills; inflation surges; approval falls
Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade; abortion rights become election battleground
Trump indicted four times (federal, Georgia, classified documents, NY fraud)
Trump wins presidential election; Republicans control Senate and House
Trump returns to White House; mass deportations begin; federal agencies dismantled
Democratic opposition fragments; constitutional challenges to executive orders mount

name: European Union Political Tensions
summary: The EU faces existential pressures from the far right, democratic backsliding in member states, Brexit aftermath, and the challenge of maintaining unity under Russia's war on Ukraine.

history.event: EU's largest expansion: 10 new members from Eastern Europe; bloc grows to 25
Eurozone debt crisis; Greece, Ireland, Portugal require bailouts; austerity imposed
Refugee crisis: 1 million+ arrive; far-right parties surge across Europe
Brexit: UK votes to leave EU; 4-year negotiation process begins
Far-right parties make gains in European Parliament elections
UK formally leaves EU; transition period begins
EU agrees €750bn COVID recovery fund; first-ever joint borrowing
Hungary and Poland challenge EU rule-of-law mechanisms; funds withheld
Russia's invasion unites EU; unprecedented sanctions; arms to Ukraine; energy crisis
Energy prices spike after Russia cuts gas; energy transition accelerated
Italy's Meloni coalition governs; far-right in power for first time since Mussolini era
European Parliament elections: far right surges; Macron calls snap French election
Hungary's Orban blocks Ukraine aid; vetoes EU positions; dubbed 'Trojan horse'
Germany faces economic stagnation; coalition instability; EU debates enlargement to Ukraine

name: China Under Xi Jinping
summary: Xi Jinping has consolidated power to a degree unseen since Mao, dismantling term limits, crushing dissent in Hong Kong and Xinjiang, and positioning China for assertive global leadership.

history.event: Deng Xiaoping launches market reforms; China begins four decades of 10%/year growth
Tiananmen Square massacre; hundreds killed; Communist Party tightens grip
China joins WTO; export-led growth accelerates
Xi Jinping becomes General Secretary; begins consolidating power
'Made in China 2025' industrial strategy launched; mass surveillance infrastructure expanded
Mass detention of Uyghur Muslims in Xinjiang begins; UN calls it 'crimes against humanity'
Hong Kong protests: millions march against extradition bill; Beijing refuses to yield
Xi removes presidential term limits; consolidates 'president for life' pathway
COVID-19 emerges in Wuhan; China suppresses early warnings; pandemic spreads globally
National Security Law imposed on Hong Kong; pro-democracy movement crushed
Xi wins unprecedented third term; 'Xi Jinping Thought' written into constitution
COVID Zero policy collapses after mass protests; abrupt reopening
Property sector crisis deepens; Evergrande liquidated; youth unemployment hits record 20%+
Military drills around Taiwan intensify; senior PLA generals purged for corruption
Economy faces deflation and demographic decline; Xi doubles down on state-led model

name: Global Inflation & Interest Rate Cycle
summary: The post-COVID inflation surge and subsequent aggressive rate hike cycle by central banks reshaped the global economy, ending the era of cheap money that had persisted since the 2008 financial crisis.

history.event: Global financial crisis; central banks cut rates to near zero; quantitative easing begins
Decade of ultra-low interest rates; asset prices boom; inflation remains subdued
COVID lockdowns cause supply shocks; $5tn+ in government stimulus injected globally
Inflation begins rising; central banks call it 'transitory'; shipping costs spike 10x
Russia invades Ukraine; energy and food prices surge globally
US inflation hits 9.1% — highest since 1981; Fed begins aggressive hike cycle
Fed raises rates 11 times from 0.25% to 5.5%; fastest hiking cycle in 40 years
UK, EU, Canada, Australia follow with their own hiking cycles
Emerging market currencies collapse; Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Argentina face debt crises
Silicon Valley Bank collapses; fears of banking crisis prompt market turmoil
Inflation falls toward 3%; Fed signals possible rate cuts ahead
Fed cuts rates for first time since 2020; ECB begins cutting cycle
Trade tariffs under Trump reignite inflation fears; rate cut expectations scaled back
Global economy navigates stagflation risks; 'higher for longer' era redefines finance

name: Global AI Race
summary: The emergence of large language models has triggered a geopolitical and commercial race for AI supremacy, reshaping industries, labour markets, and national security strategies worldwide.

history.event: AlexNet wins ImageNet; deep learning revolution begins
Google publishes 'Attention is All You Need' — transformer architecture born
GPT-1, GPT-2, GPT-3 released; OpenAI demonstrates language capabilities
China publishes 'AI 2030' plan; massive state investment in AI research
US imposes sweeping chip export controls to slow China's AI development
ChatGPT launches; reaches 100 million users in 2 months — fastest product ever
Microsoft invests $10bn in OpenAI; integrates AI into Office and Bing
GPT-4 released; passes bar exam; demonstrates multimodal capabilities
Google launches Bard; Meta releases Llama; AI arms race among tech giants accelerates
OpenAI board fires then reinstates Sam Altman; internal crisis exposes governance concerns
Nvidia becomes world's most valuable company; H100 chips become strategic commodity
DeepSeek R1 released by Chinese startup; matches GPT-4 at fraction of cost; US stocks plunge
AI agents begin replacing knowledge workers; regulatory frameworks lag capabilities
AGI debate intensifies; EU AI Act takes effect; US and China deepen parallel AI ecosystems

name: China–Taiwan Tensions
summary: Taiwan's de facto independence and China's claim of sovereignty have made the Taiwan Strait one of the world's most volatile flashpoints, with implications for global semiconductor supply chains.

history.event: Mao wins civil war; Chiang Kai-shek's Republic of China retreats to Taiwan
Korean War: US 7th Fleet deploys to Taiwan Strait; becomes de facto security guarantee
UN transfers China's seat from Taiwan to PRC; Taiwan loses diplomatic recognition
US switches recognition to PRC; Taiwan Relations Act preserves unofficial ties and arms sales
Third Taiwan Strait Crisis: China fires missiles; US sends two carrier groups
Pro-independence DPP wins Taiwan presidency for first time; China issues threats
Tsai Ing-wen elected; refuses to endorse 'One China' consensus; Beijing cuts dialogue
Tsai re-elected by landslide; military incursions into Taiwan air defence zone surge
Pelosi visits Taiwan; China launches largest-ever military exercises around island
US passes CHIPS Act partly to reduce dependence on TSMC; Taiwan makes 90% of world's advanced chips
Lai Ching-te (pro-sovereignty) wins Taiwan presidency; Beijing condemns result
China conducts 'Joint Sword-2024' exercises simulating blockade of Taiwan
Tensions remain elevated; US arms sales continue; invasion timeline debated by analysts

name: India's Geopolitical Rise
summary: India has emerged as a major global swing power, leveraging its position between the US and Russia, hosting G20, and positioning itself as the voice of the Global South amid rising great power rivalry.

history.event: Independence; partition from Pakistan; Nehru's non-aligned movement founded
India tests nuclear device ('Smiling Buddha'); international sanctions follow
Economic liberalisation begins; licence raj dismantled; GDP growth accelerates
India tests nuclear weapons; becomes declared nuclear power; US sanctions follow
US-India civil nuclear deal; strategic partnership deepens
Modi and BJP win landslide; Hindu nationalist agenda; 'Make in India' launched
Demonetisation shock: 86% of cash withdrawn overnight; economic disruption
Modi wins second term with larger majority; CAA and Kashmir moves spark protests
Galwan Valley clash: Indian and Chinese soldiers fight with clubs; 20 Indians killed
India becomes world's largest vaccine producer; diplomatic capital gained
India refuses to condemn Russia invasion; buys discounted Russian oil; maintains 'strategic autonomy'
G20 presidency; Chandrayaan-3 lunar landing; becomes world's 5th largest economy
Modi wins third term but loses parliamentary majority; coalition government formed
India becomes manufacturing alternative to China; deep infrastructure investment; digital public goods exported

name: Middle East Geopolitical Realignment
summary: Saudi Arabia and Iran's rapprochement, the Abraham Accords, and the Gaza war have dramatically reshuffled alliances in the Middle East, upending decades of US-centred regional architecture.

history.event: Iranian Revolution; US-Iran alliance becomes hostility; Saudi-Iran rivalry begins
Gulf War: US deploys to Saudi Arabia to expel Iraq from Kuwait; permanent US military presence begins
Iraq invasion topples Saddam; Iran gains regional influence; Shia-Sunni tensions intensify
Arab Spring: regimes topple in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya; Syria descends into civil war
Saudi Arabia intervenes in Yemen; Iran-backed Houthis control capital
Saudi Arabia executes Shia cleric Nimr al-Nimr; Iran protests stormed; ties severed
Abraham Accords: UAE, Bahrain, Sudan, Morocco normalise ties with Israel
US withdraws from Afghanistan; allies question US commitment to region
China brokers Saudi-Iran rapprochement; diplomatic relations restored after 7-year break
Hamas attacks Israel; Abraham Accords stall; Saudi normalisation suspended
Houthis attack Red Sea shipping; global trade routes disrupted
Iran directly attacks Israel; Israel retaliates; regional war narrowly avoided
Post-Gaza order unclear; two-state solution recedes; Gulf states hedge between US and China
